Title: Promotions List — Load and Display in Admin Blade
Preconditions: Marketing module installed; admin user authenticated at {{BACK_URL}}
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}
[BLADE] wait for Admin home blade to load
[ACT] click 'Marketing' in left navigation menu
[BLADE] wait for Marketing blade to open
[ACT] click 'Promotions' menu item
[BLADE] wait for Promotions list blade to open
Assertions:
[BLADE] blade title reads 'Promotions'
[GRID] promotions list grid is visible with at least one row
[GRID] grid columns include Name, Active status, Store, Priority, Type (or equivalent)
[DOM] 'Add' button is visible in the blade toolbar
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/marketing/promotions returns HTTP 200 with items array
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during navigation
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on promotions list load
Failure_Signals: Spinner visible >10s, 4xx/5xx on marketing API, blade never opens, console TypeError

Title: Promotion — Create New Simple Promotion (Happy Path)
Preconditions: Marketing module installed; admin user authenticated; Promotions list blade open
Steps:
[ACT] click 'Add' button in Promotions list blade toolbar
[BLADE] wait for new promotion form blade to open
[ACT] fill 'Name' field with 'QA Test Promotion {{timestamp}}'
[ACT] fill 'Description' field with 'Automated test promotion'
[ACT] set 'Active' toggle to enabled
[ACT] fill 'Priority' field with '10'
[ACT] select store from 'Store' dropdown (select first available or {{STORE_ID}})
[SAVE] click 'Save' and wait for confirmation
[WAIT] blade closes or success notification visible
Assertions:
[TOAST] success notification appears after save
[GRID] new promotion appears in Promotions list grid with correct Name
[FORM] promotion Name persisted as entered
[FORM] Active status shown as enabled
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST /api/marketing/promotions (or equivalent) returns HTTP 200/201
[API] GET /api/marketing/promotions?keyword={{name}} returns the new promotion
[ADMIN] promotion appears in list grid after save
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during create
Failure_Signals: Spinner visible >15s after save, 4xx/5xx on save API call, blade does not close, console TypeError on save
Cleanup: Delete test promotion after test via grid delete action or REST API DELETE /api/marketing/promotions/{id}

Title: Promotion Priority — Higher Priority Promotion Applied First
Preconditions: Marketing module installed; admin authenticated; two active promotions exist with same reward type but different priorities and overlap in trigger conditions
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}} → Marketing → Promotions
[BLADE] open Promotion A (higher priority, e.g. priority=10)
[ACT] note its reward type and discount value
[BLADE] open Promotion B (lower priority, e.g. priority=20)
[ACT] note its reward type and discount value
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}} and add qualifying product to cart
[WAIT] cart totals update with discount applied
Assertions:
[MATH] discount applied equals Promotion A's reward (higher priority wins)
[DOM] cart discount label references Promotion A name or applies the correct amount
[MATH] cart total = subtotal minus Promotion A discount (not both promotions stacked under BestRewardPolicy)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST /graphql cart query: items[].discounts[] contains Promotion A name; Promotion B discount absent when same reward type
[API] cart.discountTotal matches Promotion A reward amount
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during cart render
Failure_Signals: Both promotions applied simultaneously when BestRewardPolicy is active; discount amount unexpectedly small; console TypeError
Cleanup: Remove item from cart after test; no Admin cleanup needed

Title: Promotion Evaluation Policy — BestRewardPromotionPolicy vs CombineStackablePromotionPolicy
Preconditions: Store settings accessible; two overlapping cart subtotal reward promotions exist (one with coupon
Test_Data: one automatic); store evaluation policy is configurable
Steps: admin_url={{BACK_URL}}, admin_email={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, admin_password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, store_id={{STORE_ID}}
Assertions:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}} → Stores → select {{STORE_ID}} → Settings blade
[ACT] locate 'Promotion evaluation policy' setting
[ACT] set policy to 'BestRewardPromotionPolicy'
[SAVE] save store settings
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}} → add qualifying product to cart
[ACT] apply coupon code for lower-value coupon-backed promotion
[WAIT] discount applied
[ASSERT] only coupon-backed discount shown (auto discount replaced per BL-CART-003 known behavior)
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}} → set policy to 'CombineStackablePromotionPolicy'
[SAVE] save
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}} → cart → remove coupon → re-add qualifying product
[ACT] apply coupon again
[WAIT] discount recalculated
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[MATH] under BestRewardPolicy: coupon-backed reward shown even if smaller than auto reward (by design per BL-CART-003)
[MATH] under CombineStackablePolicy: only one CartSubtotalReward per priority group kept
[DOM] policy switch in Admin persisted after save
Failure_Signals:
[API] GET /api/platform/stores/{{STORE_ID}}/settings includes updated policy value
[API] POST /graphql cart query discountTotal changes between policy modes
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during policy change
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on store settings save
Cleanup: Policy value not persisted, cart discounts unchanged after policy switch, console TypeError
References: Restore original evaluation policy setting in Store settings after test

Title: REST API — GET Promotions List
Preconditions: Marketing module running; admin Bearer token obtained via /connect/token
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with admin credentials → save Bearer token
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/marketing/promotions with Authorization: Bearer {{token}} and headers Accept: application/json
Assertions:
[STATUS] HTTP 200 response
[BODY] response contains 'totalCount' field (integer >= 0)
[BODY] response contains 'results' or 'items' array
[SCHEMA] each promotion object contains id, name, isActive, storeId, priority fields
[PERF] response time < 1000ms
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[DB] at least one promotion exists in list (matches Admin Promotions blade count)
[CONSOLE] no unexpected errors
Failure_Signals: HTTP 401/403 (auth issue), HTTP 500 (server error), missing required fields in response, response time >3s

Title: GraphQL xAPI — promotionCoupons Happy Path Query
Preconditions: xMarketing experience API module deployed; at least one active, public promotion with a coupon code exists in store {{STORE_ID}}; authenticated user whose storeId matches {{STORE_ID}}
Assertions:
[ERRORS] errors[] is empty
[DATA] data.promotionCoupons.totalCount >= 0 (integer)
[DATA] items[] array present; each item has non-null id, systemName, couponCode
[DATA] all returned items have couponCode != null (promotions without coupons excluded)
[NULL] endDate is ISO 8601 string when present; null acceptable for open-ended promotions
[SCHEMA] all field names are camelCase; no unexpected extra fields
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] count of returned items ≤ totalCount (pagination consistent)
[ADMIN] count of active+public+couponed promotions in Admin for {{STORE_ID}} matches totalCount
[STOREFRONT] /account/coupons storefront page shows same coupon codes
[CONSOLE] no JS errors if tested via browser
Failure_Signals: HTTP 200 but errors[] non-empty, couponCode null on returned items, totalCount mismatch with Admin, response >500ms

Title: GraphQL xAPI — promotionCoupons Schema Introspection
Preconditions: xMarketing xAPI module deployed; GraphQL introspection enabled
Assertions:
[ERRORS] introspection returns HTTP 200, errors[] empty
[DATA] 'promotionCoupons' field exists in root Query type
[SCHEMA] accepts args: storeId (String! non-null), userId (String nullable), currencyCode (String nullable), cultureName (String nullable), first (Int nullable), after (String nullable)
[SCHEMA] return type is a connection type with totalCount (Int) and items ([PromotionCouponType])
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ADMIN] field names match Admin module structure
[CONSOLE] no introspection errors
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500 on introspection, promotionCoupons field missing from schema, storeId arg not String! non-null

Title: GraphQL xAPI — promotionCoupons Unauthenticated (Negative)
Preconditions: xMarketing xAPI module deployed
Steps: [GQL] POST {{BACK_URL}}/graphql with promotionCoupons query (storeId: {{STORE_ID}}, first: 5, after: "0") — NO Authorization header
Assertions:
[ERRORS] errors[] contains at least one error with UNAUTHORIZED or authentication-related message/code
[DATA] data.promotionCoupons is null (no promotion data returned)
[STATUS] HTTP status remains 200 (GraphQL spec — errors returned in body, not HTTP 4xx)
[SCHEMA] response body contains no stack traces or internal infrastructure paths
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no internal errors exposed
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 response (not 401/403 at transport level)
Failure_Signals: HTTP 200 with promotion data returned (security failure), HTTP 500, stack trace in response, no errors[] populated

[STATUS] HTTP 200 (GraphQL spec)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no internal paths or stack traces in errors
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 at transport level
Failure_Signals: HTTP 200 with Store B promotions returned (cross-store data bleed — security failure), HTTP 500, no errors[] populated

Title: GraphQL xAPI — promotionCoupons Missing Required storeId (Negative)

Assertions:

[SCHEMA] error location includes line/column info for the missing argument
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no unhandled exceptions

Failure_Signals: HTTP 500, no errors[] (silent failure), data returned despite missing required arg

Title: GraphQL xAPI — promotionCoupons Pagination Correctness
Preconditions: xMarketing xAPI deployed; authenticated user with storeId match; store has 3+ active public couponed promotions
Steps:
[AUTH] obtain Bearer token for {{USER_EMAIL}}
[GQL] POST /graphql: promotionCoupons(storeId: {{STORE_ID}}, first: 2, after: "0") → get page 1 items, save last cursor or use skip
[VAR] save totalCount from page 1 response
[GQL] POST /graphql: promotionCoupons(storeId: {{STORE_ID}}, first: 2, after: "2") → get page 2 items
Assertions:
[COUNT] page 1 returns exactly 2 items (or fewer if totalCount < 2)
[COUNT] totalCount on page 1 and page 2 are identical (consistent)
[DATA] page 2 items are different from page 1 items (no duplicates across pages)
[COUNT] combining page 1 and page 2 items covers the first 4 promotions without gaps
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] totalCount matches count of all active+public+couponed promotions via Admin verification
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
Failure_Signals: Pages return identical items (pagination broken), totalCount inconsistent between pages, duplicates across pages, HTTP 200 + errors[]

Title: GraphQL xAPI — promotionCoupons Filter Correctness (Only Active+Public+Couponed)
Preconditions: xMarketing xAPI deployed; authenticated admin user; setup: deactivated promotion and private promotion exist in store
Assertions:
[ERRORS] errors[] empty
[DATA] inactive promotion (isActive=false) NOT present in items[]
[DATA] private promotion (isPublic=false) NOT present in items[]
[DATA] active+public+couponed promotion IS present in items[]
[COUNT] totalCount reflects only qualifying promotions
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ADMIN] Admin Promotions list for {{STORE_ID}} filtered to Active+Public shows same count as totalCount
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
Failure_Signals: Inactive or private promotions appearing in results (filter broken), totalCount inflated by non-qualifying promotions
Cleanup: Delete any test promotions created in SETUP after test

Title: GraphQL xAPI — promotionCoupons Localization (cultureName)

[DATA] without cultureName — name, label, description return null (no default locale applied per spec)

[CONSOLE] no JS errors
Failure_Signals: name/label/description returning same value regardless of cultureName, systemName returning null, empty string instead of null for missing locale

Title: Marketing Security — RBAC: Read-Only Role Cannot Create or Delete Promotions
Preconditions: Marketing module installed; a role with only 'Marketing:read' (or 'Marketing:access') permission exists; user assigned to that role
Steps:
[AUTH] sign in to Admin SPA as a user with Marketing read-only role
[NAV] navigate to Marketing → Promotions
[BLADE] wait for Promotions list blade
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Add' button is absent or disabled for read-only user
[DOM] Delete icon/button is absent or disabled for read-only user
[BLADE] Promotions list blade is accessible (read access granted)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/marketing/promotions with read-only user Bearer token returns HTTP 403
[API] DELETE {{BACK_URL}}/api/marketing/promotions/{id} with read-only user token returns HTTP 403
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
Failure_Signals: Add or Delete button visible for read-only user (permission leak), API returns 200 for unauthorized create/delete
Cleanup: Sign out and sign back in as admin after test

Title: Marketing Security — No Data Exposure in GraphQL Error Responses
Preconditions: xMarketing xAPI deployed
Assertions:
[ERRORS] unknown field query returns errors[] with field-level error (location info present); HTTP 200
[SCHEMA] errors[] messages contain no server file paths, SQL fragments, or stack traces
[DATA] unknown field query returns no data for valid fields (partial data not leaked alongside error)
[PERF] large first value query completes in < 2000ms without timeout or memory crash
[DATA] large first value returns actual matching count (capped at real data — no artificial inflation)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no unhandled exceptions in error responses
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 at transport level for both cases
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500 on unknown field, stack trace in errors[], query times out with large first value

Title: Marketing Performance — promotionCoupons Response Time
Preconditions: xMarketing xAPI deployed; store with 20+ active public couponed promotions; authenticated user
Assertions:
[PERF] average response time < 500ms for all 3 calls
[PERF] no single call exceeds 2000ms
[DATA] all 3 responses return identical totalCount (no flapping)
[ERRORS] errors[] empty on all calls
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on all 3 calls
Failure_Signals: Any call exceeds 2000ms, totalCount fluctuates between calls, HTTP 500 on any call

Title: Marketing Performance — DataLoader Batch Loading Verification (No N+1)
Preconditions: xMarketing xAPI deployed; store with 10+ active public couponed promotions; admin with backend tracing access or network DevTools
Steps:
[AUTH] obtain admin Bearer token
[GQL] POST {{BACK_URL}}/graphql with DevTools network monitoring enabled:
promotionCoupons(storeId: {{STORE_ID}}, first: 10, after: "0") { totalCount items { id couponCode } }
[WAIT] response received
[NETWORK] inspect network requests to coupon/search APIs during the single GQL call
Assertions:
[PERF] single GraphQL request resulted in at most ONE batch call to coupon search service (DataLoader behavior — not 10 individual calls)
[DATA] response contains couponCode for each promotion item (non-null, DataLoader resolved correctly)
[PERF] total response time < 1000ms for 10 items
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no timeout errors or DataLoader exceptions
[NETWORK] no burst of identical coupon search sub-requests (N+1 pattern absent)
Failure_Signals: 10 individual coupon search calls observed instead of 1 batch call (N+1 regression), couponCode null on items, response >3000ms
